debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
β : Type u_2
G✝ H C : Graph α β
S X Y : Set α
M M' : Set β
u v x y z : α
e f : β
P P' : WList α β
hM : @Graph.IsMatching α β G✝ M
G : Graph α β
⊢ ∀ ⦃I J : Set α⦄, (∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ J ⊆ V(G, M)) → I ⊆ J → ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M)

proof:
intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ'
failed_attempts:
intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hJ
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ

intro I J hI hJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hI
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hJ hJ

intro I J hJ hIJ
    -- Extract the matching M for J
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hJ
    -- Use the same matching M for I since I ⊆ J ⊆ V(G, M)
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    -- Prove I ⊆ V(G, M) using I ⊆ J and J ⊆ V(G, M)
    exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ
  
  indep_extension := by
    sorry

obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hJ
  refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
  exact hIJ.trans hJ

intro I J hJ hI
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hI hJ'

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    have h₁ : J ⊆ V(G, M) := hJ'
    have h₂ : I ⊆ J := hIJ
    have h₃ : I ⊆ V(G, M) := h₂.trans h₁
    exact h₃

intro S hS T hT
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hS'⟩ := hS
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hT hS'

intro I J hI hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hI
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    have h : I ⊆ J := hIJ
    have h' : J ⊆ V(G, M) := hJ
    exact h.trans h'

sorry -- Prove that G.IsMatching ∅
     sorry -- Prove that ∅ ⊆ V(G, ∅)

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hJ
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    -- We already have J ⊆ V(G, M) from hJ, and I ⊆ J by hIJ.
    -- Since ⊆ is transitive, I ⊆ V(G, M).
    have hI : I ⊆ V(G, M) := hIJ.trans hJ
    exact hI

intro S hS S' hSS'
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hS⟩ := hS
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact hS.mono hSS'

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJVM⟩ := hJ
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    have hIVM : I ⊆ V(G, M) := Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJVM
    exact hIVM

intro S T hT hS
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hV⟩ := hT
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact hV.mono hS

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact hIJ.trans hJ'

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hJ
    refine ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ
successful_attempts:
intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ'

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact hIJ.trans hJ'

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJM⟩ := hJ
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJM

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    have hI : I ⊆ V(G, M) := Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ'
    exact hI

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ'

rintro I J ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ hIJ
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ
  <;> sorry

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ_subset⟩ := hJ
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact hIJ.trans hJ_subset

intro I J hJ hsub
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
    have hI : I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
      -- Since I ⊆ J and J ⊆ V(G, M), we have I ⊆ V(G, M)
      exact hsub.trans hJ'
    refine ⟨M, hM, hI⟩

intro I J hI hI_subset_J
    -- Extract the matching M for J
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hI
    -- Use the same matching M for I since I is a subset of J
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    -- Show that I is a subset of the vertices of M
    exact hI_subset_J.trans hJ

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJv⟩ := hJ
    exact ⟨M, hM, by
      have hIv : I ⊆ V(G, M) := Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJv
      exact hIv⟩

intro I J hJ hIJ
    have h_main : ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
      obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hJ
      refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
      -- Since I ⊆ J and J ⊆ V(G, M), we have I ⊆ V(G, M)
      exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ
    exact h_main

intro I J hJ hIJ
    rcases hJ with ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩
    use M
    constructor
    · exact hM
    · exact hIJ.trans hJ

intro I J hJ hI
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hJ
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    have h : I ⊆ V(G, M) := hI.trans hJ
    exact h

intro I J hJ hI
    have h_main : ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
      obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
      refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
      exact Set.Subset.trans hI hJ'
    exact h_main

intro I J hJ hIJ
    have h_main : ∃ M, G.IsMatching M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
      -- Extract the matching M such that J ⊆ V(G, M) from the hypothesis hJ
      obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ_subset⟩ := hJ
      -- Since I ⊆ J and J ⊆ V(G, M), we have I ⊆ V(G, M)
      refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
      -- Prove that I ⊆ V(G, M) using subset transitivity
      exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ_subset
    
    -- The main statement follows directly from h_main
    exact h_main

intro I J hJ hIJ
    obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ'⟩ := hJ
    refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
    exact Set.Subset.trans hIJ hJ'

have h₁ : ∀ ⦃I J : Set α⦄, (∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ J ⊆ V(G, M)) → I ⊆ J → ∃ M, @Graph.IsMatching α β G M ∧ I ⊆ V(G, M) := by
      intro I J hJ IJ
      obtain ⟨M, hM, hJ⟩ := hJ
      refine' ⟨M, hM, _⟩
      -- We already have J ⊆ V(G, M) from hJ, and I ⊆ J from IJ.
      -- Therefore, I ⊆ V(G, M) by transitivity of ⊆.
      exact Set.Subset.trans IJ hJ
    simpa using h₁
